title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement pschloss@umich.edu. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: chimera_report
text: chimera_report generated rchime() function data.frame 18 columns row sequence dataset. ’s brief description various columns.
code: 1. Score: a higher score means a more likely chimeric alignment. 2. Query: query sequence name. 3. ParentA: parent A sequence name. 4. ParentB: parent B sequence name. 5. Top_Parent: top parent sequence name (i.e. parent most similar to the query). 6. QM: percentage of similarity of query (Q) and model (M) constructed as a part of parent A and a part of parent B. 7. QA: percentage of similarity of query (Q) and parent A. 8. QB: percentage of similarity of query (Q) and parent B. 9. QAB: percentage of similarity of parent A and parent B. 10. QT percentage of similarity of query (Q) and top parent (T) 11. LY : yes votes in the left part of the model. 12. LN: no votes in the left part of the model. 13. LA: abstain votes in the left part of the model. 14. RY: yes votes in the right part of the model. 15. RN: no votes in the right part of the model. 16. RA: abstain votes in the right part of the model. 17. Div: divergence, defined as (QM - QT).  18. Chimeric_Status: query is chimeric (Y), or not (N), or is a borderline case (?).

title: chimera_report
text: Let’s run rchime() denovo approach generate chimera report, take closer look.

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
text: rchime() function allows detect remove chimeric sequences using dataset ’s reference (denovo). denovo approach preferred method removing chimeras. rchime() can used strollur objects data.frames inputs. Let’s look examples data types using sequence data mothur’s MiSeq_SOP example analysis.

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
code: fasta_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_fasta.rds")) abundance_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_abundance.rds"))  strollur <- strollur::new_dataset("rchime denovo example")  strollur::add(strollur, table = fasta_data, type = "sequence") #> Added 6084 sequences. strollur::assign(strollur, table = abundance_data, type = "sequence_abundance") #> Assigned 6084 sequence abundances.  strollur #> rchime denovo example: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs #> Minimum:         1  249    249      0        3     0      1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        4     0   3216.38 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0  32163.75 #> Median:          1  253    253      0        4     0  64327.50 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0  96491.25 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  254    254      0        6     0 125438.62 #> Maximum:         1  256    256      0        8     0 128655.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0  64327.64 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 6084  #> Total number of seqs: 128655  #>  #> Total number of samples: 20

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
code: df <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_data_frame_by_sample.rds"))  str(df) #> 'data.frame':    11039 obs. of  4 variables: #>  $ sequence_name: chr  "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10133_8460" "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10133_8460" "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10133_8460" "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10133_8460" ... #>  $ sequence     : chr  "TACGTAGGGGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGATTTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGTAGGCGGCCATGCAAGTCAGAAGTGAAAACCCGGGGCTCAACCCTGGGAGTGCTTTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ "TACGTAGGGGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGATTTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGTAGGCGGCCATGCAAGTCAGAAGTGAAAACCCGGGGCTCAACCCTGGGAGTGCTTTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ "TACGTAGGGGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGATTTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGTAGGCGGCCATGCAAGTCAGAAGTGAAAACCCGGGGCTCAACCCTGGGAGTGCTTTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ "TACGTAGGGGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGATTTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGTAGGCGGCCATGCAAGTCAGAAGTGAAAACCCGGGGCTCAACCCTGGGAGTGCTTTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ ... #>  $ sample       : chr  "F3D2" "F3D146" "F3D149" "F3D150" ... #>  $ abundance    : int  222 1 1 1 127 17 32 13 95 86 ...

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
text: removing chimeras using denovo method, potential parents chosen abundant sequences dataset. remove chimeras let’s discuss dereplicate parameter. dereplicate=FALSE, sequence flagged chimeric one sample, removed samples. experience suggests bit aggressive since ’ve seen rare sequences get flagged chimeric ’re abundant sequence another sample. conservative approach, recommend using default dereplicate=TRUE remove sequences samples flagged chimeric. Let’s use denovo method remove chimeras.
code: strollur_results <- rchime(strollur) #> ℹ The denovo method runs with a single processor. #> Added a chimera_report report. #> → rchime removed `10453` chimeras from your dataset. #> → It took `7.81381177902222` seconds to detect and remove the chimeras.  strollur #> rchime denovo example: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs #> Minimum:         1  249    249      0        3     0      1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        4     0   2955.05 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0  29550.50 #> Median:          1  253    253      0        4     0  59101.00 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0  88651.50 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  254    254      0        6     0 115246.95 #> Maximum:         1  256    256      0        8     0 118202.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0  59101.14 #>  #> scrap_summary: #>       type      trash_code unique total #> 1 sequence rchime-chimeras   3588 10453 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 2496  #> Total number of seqs: 118202  #>  #> Total number of samples: 20  #> Total number of custom reports: 1  data_frame_results <- rchime(df) #> ℹ The denovo method runs with a single processor. #> → rchime detected `10453` chimeras in your dataset. #> → It took `7.80556631088257` seconds to detect the chimeras.

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
text: rchime() function returns list containing results function. running command strollur object, chimera_report added, chimeras removed automatically. Let’s take closer look results returned.

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
text: chimera_report data.frame row sequence dataset. Let’s take look first 5 chimeric sequences report:

title: Denovo Chimera Detection

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
text: Results contain set_abundance_values list dereplicate = TRUE removing chimeras automatically. set_abundance_values three items: ‘sequence_names’, ‘abundances’ ‘samples’. sequence dataset entry sequence_names abundances. abundance values parsed sample, order given set_abundance_values$samples. Let’s look first two sequences abundances detecting chimeras: can see abundances sequence M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10133_8460 remain meaning chimeric sample present . can see sequence M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10134_24617 found chimeric every sample included . Now let’s look example sequence found chimeric samples present . can see samples F3D0 F3D142 find sequence chimeric, F3D1, F3D2, F3D148 find chimeric abundance samples set 0.

title: Reference Based Chimera Detection
text: rchime() function allows detect remove chimeras dataset using referenced based approach. rchime() can used strollur objects data.frames inputs. Let’s look examples data types using sequence data mothur’s MiSeq_SOP example analysis, silva_gold() reference sequences. rchime designed flexible can use reference choose.

title: Reference Based Chimera Detection
text: Let’s create strollur object containing MiSeq_SOP sequences.
code: fasta_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_fasta.rds")) abundance_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_abundance.rds"))  strollur <- strollur::new_dataset("rchime reference example")  strollur::add(strollur, table = fasta_data, type = "sequence") #> Added 6084 sequences. strollur::assign(strollur, table = abundance_data, type = "sequence_abundance") #> Assigned 6084 sequence abundances.  strollur #> rchime reference example: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs #> Minimum:         1  249    249      0        3     0      1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        4     0   3216.38 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0  32163.75 #> Median:          1  253    253      0        4     0  64327.50 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0  96491.25 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  254    254      0        6     0 125438.62 #> Maximum:         1  256    256      0        8     0 128655.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0  64327.64 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 6084  #> Total number of seqs: 128655  #>  #> Total number of samples: 20

title: Reference Based Chimera Detection
code: data_df <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_data_frame.rds"))  str(data_df) #> 'data.frame':    6084 obs. of  3 variables: #>  $ sequence_name: chr  "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10133_8460" "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10134_24617" "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10331_23332" "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10340_12294" ... #>  $ sequence     : chr  "TACGTAGGGGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGATTTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGTAGGCGGCCATGCAAGTCAGAAGTGAAAACCCGGGGCTCAACCCTGGGAGTGCTTTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ "TACGTAGGGGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGATTTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGTAGGCGGCCATGCAAGTCAGAAGTGAAAACCCGGGGCTCAACCCTGGGAGTGCTTTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ "TACGGAGGATGCGAGCGTTATCCGGATTTATTGGGTTTAAAGGGAGCGCAGGCGGCATGGCAAGTCAGATGTGAAAGCCCGGGGCTCAACCCCGGGACTGCATTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ "TACGGAGGATGCGAGCGTTATCCGGATTTATTGGGTTTAAAGGGTGCGTAGGCGGGCTGTTAAGTCAGCGGTCAAATGTCGGGGCTCAACGCCGTCGAGCCGTTGAAACTG"| __truncated__ ... #>  $ abundance    : int  620 4 1 1 1 1 1 3 1 2 ...

title: Reference Based Chimera Detection
text: removing chimeras using reference, potential parents chosen set reference sequences. Let’s use reference remove chimeras.
code: reference <- silva_gold() str(reference) #> 'data.frame':    5181 obs. of  2 variables: #>  $ sequence_name: chr  "7000004128189528" "7000004128189537" "7000004128189547" "7000004128189554" ... #>  $ sequence     : chr  "GACGAACGCTGGCGGCGTGCTTAACACATGCAAGTCGAGCGGAAAGGCCCTTCGGGGTACTCGAGCGGCGAACGGGTGAGTAACACGTGGGCAACCTACCCCCAGCACCGG"| __truncated__ "GATGAACGCTGGCGGTATGCTTAACACATGCAAGTCGAACGGAATCTTCGGATTTAGTGGCGGACGGGTGAGTAACGCGTGAGAATCTAGCTCTAGGTCGGGGACAACCAC"| __truncated__ "ATTGAACGCTGGCGGCATGCCTTACACATGCAAGTCGAACGGTAACAGGTCTTCGGATGCTGACGAGTGGCGAACGGGTGAGTAATACATCGGAACGTGCCCGATCGTGGG"| __truncated__ "GATGAACGCTGGCGGCGTGCCTAATACATGCAAGTCGAACGAAGCATCTTCGGATGCTTAGTGGCGAACGGGTGAGTAACACGTAGATAACCTACCTTTAACTCGAGGATA"| __truncated__ ...  strollur_results <- rchime(strollur, reference = reference) #> Added a chimera_report report. #> → rchime removed `1037` chimeras from your dataset. #> → It took `11.7162761688232` seconds to detect and remove the chimeras.  strollur #> rchime reference example: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs #> Minimum:         1  249    249      0        3     0      1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        4     0   3190.45 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0  31904.50 #> Median:          1  253    253      0        4     0  63809.00 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0  95713.50 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  254    254      0        6     0 124427.55 #> Maximum:         1  256    256      0        8     0 127618.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0  63809.14 #>  #> scrap_summary: #>       type      trash_code unique total #> 1 sequence chimeras_rchime    787  1037 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 5297  #> Total number of seqs: 127618  #>  #> Total number of samples: 20  #> Total number of custom reports: 1  data_frame_results <- rchime(data_df, reference = reference) #> → rchime detected `1037` chimeras in your dataset. #> → It took `11.6554758548737` seconds to detect the chimeras.

title: Detect and Remove Chimeras from Amplicon Sequence Analysis Data
text: can install development version rchime GitHub :
code: pak::pak("mothur/rchime")

title: Detect and Remove Chimeras from Amplicon Sequence Analysis Data
text: rchime() function accepts strollur objects data.frames inputs. Let’s create strollur::strollur object using files mothur’s Miseq_SOP example analysis. use denovo method rchime() detect remove chimeras dataset.
code: fasta_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_fasta.rds")) abundance_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_abundance.rds"))  data <- strollur::new_dataset("rchime denovo example")  strollur::add(data, table = fasta_data, type = "sequence") #> Added 6084 sequences. strollur::assign(data, table = abundance_data, type = "sequence_abundance") #> Assigned 6084 sequence abundances.  chimera_report <- rchime(data) #> ℹ The denovo method runs with a single processor. #> Added a chimera_report report. #> → rchime removed `10453` chimeras from your dataset. #> → It took `4.23106694221497` seconds to detect and remove the chimeras.  data #> rchime denovo example: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs #> Minimum:         1  249    249      0        3     0      1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        4     0   2955.05 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0  29550.50 #> Median:          1  253    253      0        4     0  59101.00 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0  88651.50 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  254    254      0        6     0 115246.95 #> Maximum:         1  256    256      0        8     0 118202.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0  59101.14 #>  #> scrap_summary: #>       type      trash_code unique total #> 1 sequence rchime-chimeras   3588 10453 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 2496  #> Total number of seqs: 118202  #>  #> Total number of samples: 20  #> Total number of custom reports: 1

what: Detect chimeras in your data.frames. — rchime.data.frame
title: Detect chimeras in your data.frames. — rchime.data.frame
text: rchime() function allows detect chimeras data using denovo approach alternatively reference model. preferred way use abundant sequences reference (denovo). function uses code vsearch tools.

title: Detect chimeras in your data.frames. — rchime.data.frame
code: # S3 method for class 'data.frame' rchime(   data,   reference = NULL,   dereplicate = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE,   remove_chimeras = NULL,   rchime_options = NULL,   table_names = list(sequence_name = "sequence_name", sequence = "sequence", abundance =     "abundance", sample = "sample") )

title: Detect chimeras in your data.frames. — rchime.data.frame
text: data data.frame containing sequence data. reference data.frame containing reference sequence data. dereplicate logical. dereplicate option allows flag chimeras sample. dereplicate=FALSE, sequence flagged chimeric one sample, removed samples. experience suggests bit aggressive since ’ve seen rare sequences get flagged chimeric ’re abundant sequence another sample. conservative approach, recommend using default dereplicate=TRUE remove sequences samples flagged chimeric. verbose logical, allow console outputs. Default = TRUE. remove_chimeras used data strollur object. rchime_options List, can fine tune vsearch specific options using  [rchime_options()] function. Default = NULL. table_names, named list used indicate names columns data.frame. used data data.frame. default: table_names <- list(sequence_name = "sequence_name", sequence = "sequence" abundance = "abundance", sample = "sample") table_names, 'sequence_name' string containing name column 'table' contains sequence names. Default column name 'sequence_name'. table_names, 'sequence' string containing name column 'table' contains sequences. Default column name 'sequence'. table_names, 'abundance' string containing name column 'table' contains abundances. Default column name 'abundance'. table_names, 'sample' string containing name column 'table' contains samples. Default column name 'sample'.

title: Detect chimeras in your data.frames. — rchime.data.frame
text: list() containing chimera report, vector chimeric sequence's names. running multiple samples dereplicate = TRUE, table containing modified sequence abundances also returned.

title: Detect and remove chimeras from your strollur object or data.frame using a denovo approach or alternatively a reference model. — rchime
code: # Let's use a strollur object with 500 sequences  data <- strollur::load_dataset(   rchime_example("strollur_multi_sample_small.rds") )  chimera_report <- rchime(data) #> ℹ The denovo method runs with a single processor. #> Added a chimera_report report. #> → rchime removed `128` chimeras from your dataset. #> → It took `0.522298097610474` seconds to detect and remove the chimeras. data #> miseq_multi_sample - 500: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns numseqs #> Minimum:         1  250    250      0        3     0    1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        4     0  167.20 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0 1672.00 #> Median:          1  253    253      0        4     0 3344.00 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0 5016.00 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  253    253      0        6     0 6520.80 #> Maximum:         1  255    255      0        6     0 6688.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0 3344.14 #>  #> scrap_summary: #>       type      trash_code unique total #> 1 sequence rchime-chimeras     90   128 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 410  #> Total number of seqs: 6688  #>  #> Total number of samples: 20  #> Total number of custom reports: 1  #>

what: Detect and remove chimeras from your strollur dataset object. — rchime.strollur
title: Detect and remove chimeras from your strollur dataset object. — rchime.strollur
text: rchime() function allows detect remove chimeras data using denovo approach alternatively reference model. preferred way use abundant sequences reference (denovo). function uses code vsearch tools.

title: Detect and remove chimeras from your strollur dataset object. — rchime.strollur
code: # S3 method for class 'strollur' rchime(   data,   reference = NULL,   dereplicate = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE,   remove_chimeras = TRUE,   rchime_options = NULL,   table_names = list(sequence_name = "sequence_name", sequence = "sequence") )

title: Detect and remove chimeras from your strollur dataset object. — rchime.strollur
text: data strollur dataset object containing sequence data. reference strollur dataset object data.frame containing reference sequence data. dereplicate logical. dereplicate option allows remove chimeras sample. dereplicate=FALSE, sequence flagged chimeric one sample, removed samples. experience suggests bit aggressive since ’ve seen rare sequences get flagged chimeric ’re abundant sequence another sample. conservative approach, recommend using default dereplicate=TRUE remove sequences samples flagged chimeric. verbose logical, allow console outputs. Default = TRUE. remove_chimeras logical, remove chimeras dataset. Default = TRUE. rchime_options List, can fine tune vsearch specific options using  [rchime_options()] function. Default = NULL. table_names, used reference data.frame. default: table_names <- list(sequence_name = "sequence_name", sequence = "sequence") table_names, 'sequence_name' string containing name column 'table' contains sequence names. Default column name 'sequence_name'. table_names, 'sequence' string containing name column 'table' contains sequences. Default column name 'sequence'.

title: Detect and remove chimeras from your strollur dataset object. — rchime.strollur
code: # Let's load a strollur object with 500 sequences  data <- strollur::load_dataset(   rchime_example("strollur_multi_sample_small.rds") )  # Detect and remove chimeras from the dataset using denovo approach by sample # (recommended)  chimera_report <- rchime(data) #> ℹ The denovo method runs with a single processor. #> Added a chimera_report report. #> → rchime removed `128` chimeras from your dataset. #> → It took `0.503233432769775` seconds to detect and remove the chimeras. data #> miseq_multi_sample - 500: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns numseqs #> Minimum:         1  250    250      0        3     0    1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        4     0  167.20 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0 1672.00 #> Median:          1  253    253      0        4     0 3344.00 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0 5016.00 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  253    253      0        6     0 6520.80 #> Maximum:         1  255    255      0        6     0 6688.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0 3344.14 #>  #> scrap_summary: #>       type      trash_code unique total #> 1 sequence rchime-chimeras     90   128 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 410  #> Total number of seqs: 6688  #>  #> Total number of samples: 20  #> Total number of custom reports: 1  #>

what: The rchime_options() function allows you to set vsearch specific optional parameters. — rchime_options
title: The rchime_options() function allows you to set vsearch specific optional parameters. — rchime_options
text: rchime_options() function allows set parameters various chimera detection functions. exception dereplicate, recommend using defaults, unless compelling reason otherwise.

title: The rchime_options() function allows you to set vsearch specific optional parameters. — rchime_options
code: rchime_options(   processors = parallelly::availableCores(),   dereplicate = TRUE,   abskew = 2,   minh = 0.28,   mindiv = 0.8,   xn = 8,   dn = 1.4,   maxp = 3 )

title: The rchime_options() function allows you to set vsearch specific optional parameters. — rchime_options
text: processors Integer, number cores use. Default = available dereplicate, Boolean, dereplicate option allows remove chimeras sample. example, dereplicate parameter FALSE, one group finds sequence chimeric, removed groups. dereplicate set TRUE, sequences found chimeric removed sample found chimeric . Default = TRUE. abskew Float, minimum abundance skew (denovo ). Default = 2.0. abskew <- min (abund(parent1), abund(parent2)) / abund(query) minh Float, Mininum score report chimera. Default 0.28. Values 0.1 5 might reasonable. Lower values increase sensitivity may report false positives. decrease –xn, may need increase –minh, vice versa. mindiv Float, Minimum divergence ratio, default 0.8. Div ratio 100%% %%identity query sequence closest candidate parent. care close chimeras, increase –mindiv , say, 1.0 2.0, also decrease –min h, say 0.1, increase sensitivity. well works depend data. Best tune parameters good benchmark. xn Float, Weight vote, also called beta parameter. Default 8.0. Decreasing weight around 3 4 may give better performance denoised data. dn Float, Pseudo-count prior number votes. Default 1.4. Probably good reason change unless can retune good benchmark data. Reasonable values probably range 0.2 2. maxp Integer, Maximum number candidate parents consider. Default 3.
